slides.title: The tiny molecule that *powers your cells*
slides.sub: CoQ10 sits at the heart of how your body makes energy. Here is why it quietly fades with age.

slides.title: Where your energy is made
slides.body: Inside every cell, mitochondria turn food and oxygen into ATP, the *fuel for everything* you do, from heartbeats to clear thinking.

slides.title: CoQ10 carries the spark
slides.body: In the electron transport chain, CoQ10 *shuttles electrons* that let your mitochondria finish making ATP. Without it, the line stalls.

slides.title: Levels fall with age
slides.body: Tissue CoQ10 tends to *drop as we get older*, and the heart, one of the most energy-hungry organs, can feel it first.

slides.title: Statins can lower it too
slides.body: Statins block the same pathway your body uses to make CoQ10, which *may help explain* the muscle fatigue some people notice.

slides.title: Support the energy engine
slides.body: Sardines, organ meats and a steady CoQ10 intake can support levels, but real energy also rests on *sleep, movement and food*.

slides.title: Energy you can *actually track*.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns your sleep, food, movement and steps into one daily Health Score, so you can see what is fueling your energy and what is draining it.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Your energy isn't a mood. It's a chemistry happening inside billions of tiny mitochondria right now. ⚡🌿

CoQ10 helps those engines turn food and oxygen into ATP, and it can quietly decline with age and with statins. The fix is supporting the whole system: sleep, food and movement. Save this for later. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those habits into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: CoQ10: The Molecule That Powers Your Cells
